description: Approved interactive deviations from the canonical rule set (roadmap 5.2). Each waiver names the rule it waives, the scope it covers, the human approver, a rationale, and an expiry date. Unexpired waivers downgrade matching findings to reported-waived without disabling the validator; an expired waiver is itself an error until renewed or removed. Waivers are granted only by the editorial owner, normally during Batch 5.3 triage of the contradiction register.

waivers.rationale: Owner decision D3 (2026-07-16): negated usage — 'I'm not the same person' asserts differentiation, which canon endorses.
Owner decision D3 (2026-07-16): negated usage — 'I'm not the same person' asserts differentiation, which canon endorses.
Owner decision D3 (2026-07-16): epistemic-doubt usage — the narrator questions self-continuity within one thread ('I couldn't know if I was the same consciousness'), canon-consistent.
Owner decision D3 (2026-07-16): continuity-verification framing in the resist branch interrogates identity claims rather than asserting sameness; waived without a close read per the owner's call.
Owner decision D3 (2026-07-16): waived per the owner's call covering the ambiguous quick-read items.
Owner decision D3 (2026-07-16): waived per the owner's call covering the ambiguous quick-read items (character dialogue expressing doubt about own upload continuity).

Owner-approved release-review triage (D9, 2026-07-16): screening hit is a canon-consistent negation, interrogation, or transformation framing (e.g. explicit 'Not escape from death. Transformation of relationship with mortality.' anti-transcendence form, binary-rejecting or question-form usage), not an assertion of the forbidden move or binary.
